story.iWant: to implement a centralized authorization service for project resources
story.soThat: all API endpoints enforce consistent role-based access control
story.tasks: - Create AuthorizationService class and exception (AC: #1-#9) - Implement getUserRoleInProject() method (AC: #1, #2) - Implement requireProjectAccess() method (AC: #3, #4, #5, #8, #9) - Implement requireThreadAccess() method (AC: #6, #7) - Implement requireThreadModify() method (AC: #6, #7) - Add global exception handler for AccessDeniedException (AC: #8) - Add logging and observability (AC: #9) - Integration with existing controllers (AC: #1-#9)

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: getUserRoleInProject(projectId, userId) returns 'viewer', 'editor', 'owner', or null (not member)
Expired memberships (expires_at < current time) return null from getUserRoleInProject()
requireProjectAccess(projectId, userId, 'editor') allows editor/owner roles only (denies viewer)
requireProjectAccess(projectId, userId, 'owner') allows owner role only
requireThreadAccess() for personal thread checks owner_id == userId
requireThreadAccess() for project thread delegates to requireProjectAccess() with 'viewer' role
AccessDeniedException thrown when insufficient permissions (caught by controller, returns 403 with clear message)
Authorization decisions logged with user ID, project ID, role, and grant/deny result

artifacts.docs: <doc path="docs/sprint-artifacts/tech-spec-epic-1.md" title="Epic 1 Technical Specification" section="Security - Role-Based Access Control (RBAC)" snippet="Viewer role: Read-only access to project threads, workflows, members list. Editor role: Viewer + create threads, execute workflows, upload files. Owner role: Editor + manage members, update project metadata, delete project. Role hierarchy enforced: owner > editor > viewer. Expired memberships (expires_at < current time) denied access." /> <doc path="docs/sprint-artifacts/tech-spec-epic-1.md" title="Epic 1 Technical Specification" section="Workflows §E1-S5 (lines 381-393)" snippet="1. Create AuthorizationService class. 2. Create AccessDeniedException custom exception. 3. Implement getUserRoleInProject() → Query project_members, check expires_at. 4. Implement requireProjectAccess() → Get role, compare hierarchy (viewer < editor < owner), throw if insufficient. 5. Implement requireThreadAccess() → Check thread type, if project → delegate to requireProjectAccess(). 6. Implement requireThreadModify() → Check ownership/role based on thread type. 7. Add request-scoped caching for role lookups. 8. Inject AuthorizationService into all controllers. 9. Add @PreAuthorize or manual checks before sensitive operations." />

artifact.reason: Existing auth service unit tests in Desktop - reference for test patterns, though Desktop auth is different (OAuth flow) than backend RBAC
Auth store tests - reference for authorization state management patterns

story-context.constraints: - **Repository Separation:** Moku API backend is SEPARATE repository. AuthorizationService implementation is in Moku API codebase, NOT in this desktop project. - **Centralized Authorization:** Single source of truth for all permission checks. Controllers delegate to AuthorizationService (never check permissions directly). All permission logic centralized in service for consistency and auditability. - **Role Hierarchy:** Numeric comparison: view=1, edit=2, admin=3. requireProjectAccess(minRole) checks if userRoleLevel >= minRoleLevel. Role hierarchy: admin > edit > view. Admin can do everything, edit can do view+create, view is read-only. - **Expired Memberships:** expires_at field in project_members. If expires_at < LocalDateTime.now(), treat as not member (return null from getUserRoleInProject()). Note: User account expiration (inactive logins) handled by Moku authentication layer, not AuthorizationService. - **Request-Scoped Caching:** Add @Scope("request") to AuthorizationService to cache role lookups within single HTTP request. Prevents repeated DB queries for same user/project in one request. Cache invalidated between requests automatically. - **Personal vs. Project Threads:** Personal threads: owner_id must match userId. Project threads: User must be project member with appropriate role. requireThreadAccess() requires view+ role. requireThreadModify() requires edit+ role (or owner for personal). - **Exception Handling:** AccessDeniedException thrown when insufficient permissions. Global @ControllerAdvice catches exception and returns 403 Forbidden with standardized error format: {timestamp, status, error, message, errorCode: "INSUFFICIENT_PERMISSIONS", path}. - **Logging and Observability:** Log all authorization decisions (granted/denied) with context (userId, projectId/threadId, role, requiredRole, decision). Use SLF4J logger. Add Micrometer metrics: Counter for auth.access_granted and auth.access_denied events. - **Thread Not Found Handling:** requireThreadAccess(threadId, userId) should throw NotFoundException if thread doesn't exist (before checking permissions). Use ThreadRepository.findById().orElseThrow(() -> new NotFoundException("Thread not found")). - **Dependency on E1-S1:** Database schema changes from E1-S1 must be applied first (V2.2 migration creates project_members table with role, expires_at columns). Verify E1-S1 is complete before starting. - **Dependency on E1-S2/S3/S4:** AuthorizationService is used by ThreadController, MessageController, ProjectController. This story should be implemented BEFORE or IN PARALLEL with E1-S2/S3/S4. If implementing after, E1-S2/S3/S4 may have stub authorization checks that need to be replaced.
story-context.interfaces: <interface name="AuthorizationService.getUserRoleInProject()" kind="Java method" signature="public String getUserRoleInProject(UUID projectId, UUID userId) - Returns 'view', 'edit', 'admin', or null if not member. Checks expires_at < now." path="Moku API - moku-api/src/main/java/com/holokai/moku/services/AuthorizationService.java" />

tests.standards: Use JUnit 5 for test structure. Use Mockito 5.x for mocking ProjectMemberRepository and ThreadRepository. Test all role hierarchy combinations (view/edit/admin × view/edit/admin requirements). Test expired membership handling (expires_at < now → treated as non-member). Test personal vs. project thread authorization paths. Test AccessDeniedException caught and returns 403. Test authorization decisions logged with correct fields.
tests.locations: moku-api/src/test/java/com/holokai/moku/ (unit and integration tests for Moku API backend)
tests.ideas: <test ac="2" idea="Unit test: getUserRoleInProject() with expired membership (expires_at < LocalDateTime.now()), verify returns null" />
